data.title: Dialed Moods in Costa Mesa innovates biohacking wellness with plant-powered supplements for optimal physical and mental performance.
data.description: A health and wellness company based in Costa Mesa, California, Dialed Moods, announced plans to broaden its focus in 2026 to include holistic biohacking using plant-powered formulas. This initiative aims to allow individuals to easily incorporate biohacking into their daily routines with the help of clean and readily available supplements. Dialed Moods' range of natural supplements is tailored to assist consumers in enhancing physical performance, mental acuity, and overall well-being. Each product is meticulously formulated with selected botanical ingredients, offering a comprehensive approach to improving health.

data.title: Societe Generale provided details on transactions conducted as part of a share buy-back program. #CompanyNews #StockBuyback
data.description: In recent news, the unemployment rate in the country has reached a new low of 3.8%, marking a significant decrease from the previous quarter. Additionally, the economy has seen a growth of 4.5% in the last fiscal year, indicating a positive trend in the overall financial landscape. Experts predict that this growth will continue in the upcoming months, leading to a more stable and prosperous economic environment. The government has also announced an increase in infrastructure spending, with a budget allocation of $1.2 billion towards key projects aimed at boosting job creation and supporting long-term economic growth. These developments point towards a promising outlook for the country's economy in the near future.

data.title: TalentSmartEQ's report shows human skills crucial in AI economy's performance. 2026 State of EQ Report released.
data.description: A new report on emotional intelligence solutions has been released by the top global provider in San Diego. The "2026 State of EQ Report" sheds light on the current state of emotional intelligence, vital for AI advancements.

data.title: Topcon and Fixposition join forces on integrating technology for future products, providing high-precision GNSS solutions worldwide.
data.description: A collaboration has been announced between Topcon Positioning Systems and Fixposition in Livermore, California. The agreement aims to combine their expertise to develop advanced technology for upcoming product releases. Topcon is known for delivering precise GNSS positioning solutions for various industries, including geomatics, construction, and agriculture on a global scale. By partnering with Fixposition, renowned for their specialization in Visual RTK and visual-aided positioning, Topcon seeks to enhance its offerings and provide additional value to its customers. This collaboration signifies a strategic move towards innovation in the field, leveraging the strengths of both companies to drive progress.

data.title: HELLENiQ ENERGY & Chevron sign agreements with Hellenic Republic for offshore exploration in Greece. Chevron holds 70% interest.
data.description: In Athens, Greece, HELLENiQ ENERGY and Chevron have signed Lease Agreements with the Hellenic Republic for exploring four offshore blocks in southern Crete and the Peloponnese. This marks a key step in Greece's upstream development. In the consortium, Chevron holds a 70% stake as the Operator, while HELLENiQ ENERGY has a 30% interest. They secured this opportunity after a competitive international tender by the Greek State.

data.title: Tom Pritzker retires as Hyatt executive chairman after 22 years; won't seek reelection, citing importance of good stewardship.
data.description: In a recent announcement, Thomas J. Pritzker, aged 75, the Executive Chairman of Hyatt Hotels Corporation (NYSE: H), has revealed his decision to retire from his position with immediate effect. He also stated that he will not be seeking reelection to the Board at the 2026 Annual Meeting of Stockholders. In a letter shared with the Hyatt Board, Pritzker emphasized the importance of good stewardship in his role and highlighted the significance of overseeing a smooth transition. This move marks a significant change in leadership within the company.

data.title: Voltalia SA reports total number of shares and voting rights in share capital as of January 31, 2026 according to French regulations.
data.description: As of January 31, 2026, the total number of shares and voting rights in the company's share capital is reported in accordance with Articles L. 233-8 (II) of the French Commercial Code and 223-16 of the General Regulations of the French Financial Markets Authority.

data.title: AI spending surge causes tech selloff, small-cap rise in 2026. SP 500 breaks even; Magnificent 7 stocks down 7.3%. Meta's $27B data center project hidden, raising concerns on tech giants' AI investment. Ernst & Young calls out accounting complexity. Tech firms investing heavily in AI, chips, and data centers spark investor anxiety on margin compression.
data.description: The market has shifted from a tech-dominated to a less stable ground in 2026, with the S&P 500 breaking even and Magnificent 7 stocks recording a 7.3% negative return. Surging AI capital expenditures have raised investor concerns on future margins, despite companies like Amazon emphasizing operational efficiencies. A recent audit reveals Meta's $27 billion data center project kept off the balance sheet through a joint venture, leading to questions about leverage and infrastructure risks not reflected in reported numbers. Tech giants investing billions in AI, chips, and data centers are now facing potential margin compression and disruptions.
